data.body:
Severity: high (when misconfigured). Source: TYB-12-followup security audit.

Where: server/internal/httpserver/checkout_handlers.go:409-421 (publicBaseURL), server/internal/httpserver/printful_handlers.go:447-463 (publicFileURL).

What: Both helpers fall back to r.Host (with X-Forwarded-Proto) when PUBLIC_BASE_URL is empty. The result is used to build (a) Stripe success_url/cancel_url and (b) the absolute file URL persisted on the Printful sync_product (which Printful re-fetches at fulfillment time).

Impact: Anyone able to send a request with an arbitrary Host header (curl, no browser CORS in the way) can:
- create a Stripe Checkout Session whose success_url points at evil.com/?session_id=…, then social-engineer a victim into completing payment — post-pay redirect lands on attacker's site (open redirect FROM a Stripe domain — high-value phishing primitive);
- pre-create a Printful sync_product for a hash a future legitimate user is about to design, with files[].url = https://evil.com/api/files/{hash}.png. Because CreateOrFetchSyncProduct does GET-first by external_id (12-hex-prefix of the hash), the attacker's poisoned product wins durably; when the customer pays, the order references that sync_variant whose print file is attacker-controlled.

Recommendation: treat PUBLIC_BASE_URL as required when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY or PRINTFUL_TOKEN is set — fail boot rather than warn. Strip the r.Host fallback for these two functions. Optionally validate r.Host against a configured allowlist for the static handler.

Out of scope: deployment ergonomics (i.e., what to set PUBLIC_BASE_URL to). Operator concern.

Implementation done in 2 commits on server-setup:
- 6ec4351 — boot guard (validatePublicBaseURL) + DI refactor: PublicBaseURL moved from PrintfulSetup to Handlers; publicFileURL/publicBaseURL rewritten as trivial formatters (no *http.Request); buildPrintful/buildStripe take their tokens as params; deleted TestPublicFileURLFallsBackToHost; added TestPublicFileURLIgnoresHostHeader, trailing-slash test pair, and four-case validatePublicBaseURL matrix in cmd/server.
- 4c4d3ea — docs: .env.example and README updated to flag PUBLIC_BASE_URL as required when Stripe/Printful are configured, plus rationale.

Manual smoke (4 cases, env vars stripped via env -i): no upstreams + no base = boots with render-only line; STRIPE_SECRET_KEY only = exits 1 with the security message; PRINTFUL_TOKEN only = exits 1 with the security message; all set + base = boots cleanly. go build/test/vet all green.

No deviations. HTTPS-prefix and broader Host-header hardening flagged in plan as out-of-scope; left a TODO near the boot guard for the live-mode HTTPS check follow-up.

Verdict: pass.

What was reviewed: commits 6ec4351 + 4c4d3ea (TYB-15) on server-setup. Read plan, full diff (8 files), every touched file in full, ran go vet/go test/all 4 smoke scenarios, grepped for residual r.Host / Printful.PublicBaseURL.

Findings: none warranting rework. Trivial nit only — main.go has a 2-line TODO at L72-73 deferring HTTPS-prefix-on-live to a follow-up; plan called this out as flag-for-human, so leaving in place. No emoji, no narration-comments, no shims, no out-of-scope creep. Both commits prefixed TYB-15.

Acceptance criteria walkthrough:
1. Stripe set + empty base -> exit non-zero with clear message: PASS (verified: 'PUBLIC_BASE_URL is required when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY or PRINTFUL_TOKEN is set...', exit 1).
2. Printful set + empty base -> exit non-zero: PASS (same message, exit 1).
3. Both set + empty base -> exit non-zero: PASS (covered by validatePublicBaseURL test 'both_configured_no_base,_blocked').
4. PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://example.com -> publicBaseURL()=='https://example.com' and publicFileURL('abc')=='https://example.com/api/files/abc.png': PASS (TestPublicFileURLPrefersConfigured + TestPublicBaseURLTrailingSlash).
5. Trailing-slash equivalence: PASS (TestPublicFileURLTrailingSlash + TestPublicBaseURLTrailingSlash).
6. Render-only mode boots with empty base: PASS (smoke verified: server boots, logs 'render path only', /healthz returns ok).
7. go test ./... green: PASS (all packages ok, go vet clean).
8. validatePublicBaseURL four-case matrix: PASS (actually 7 cases including both-with-base and individual-with-base — exceeds spec).
9. Host header cannot influence URL: PASS (TestPublicFileURLIgnoresHostHeader; signature change makes it mechanically impossible).

Implementation hotspots verified:
- main.go L57-72: log.Fatalf via logger.Fatalf, runs before listener bind. validatePublicBaseURL is package-private (lowercase v), unit-testable.
- buildPrintful/buildStripe both refactored to accept token as parameter; the old WARNING block in buildPrintful is gone (confirmed by reading current file).
- PublicBaseURL field removed from PrintfulSetup (printful_handlers.go:108-110), now on Handlers (handlers.go:50). Stripe helper no longer reaches into Printful struct.
- Grep for r.Host across server/: only hit is the regression test's hostile-Host construction (intentional, never reaches the helper).
- Both helpers use strings.TrimRight(..., '/') consistently.
- Call sites updated: checkout_handlers.go:162 + 196, printful_handlers.go:181 + 388 all drop the *http.Request arg.
- newTestHandlers, newCheckoutHandlers, newWebhookHandlers all set h.PublicBaseURL on Handlers (not on Printful).
- Docs: .env.example and README.md both explain the WHY (Host-header attacker-controllable, durable Printful poisoning, Stripe redirect hijack), not just the what. main.go package doc updated.

Verdict: pass. Moving to done.
